jokes.text: Fry did it! / Did what? / Just practicing for later.
jokes.setup: Bender preemptively blames Fry before anything happens
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's practicing blaming Fry for future accidents
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bender's opportunistic blame-shifting is both economical and character-specific

jokes.text: What's the matter compressor? / Nothing's the matter, Fry, now that I've turbocharged the matter compressor.
jokes.setup: Fry asks what the matter compressor is
jokes.punchline: Professor misinterprets as 'what's the matter' with compressor
jokes.explanation: Classic misunderstanding wordplay, serviceable but predictable

jokes.text: Yes, but I feel the nafety for safety.
jokes.setup: Professor says he feels 'the need for speed'
jokes.punchline: Leela mirrors the structure with a safety-focused pun
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows character contrast - Leela's practical nature vs Professor's recklessness

jokes.text: Oh, fuff! I don't get all up in your business when you upgrade your bazooms! / That's because my bazooms don't put anyone's life in danger!
jokes.setup: Professor defends his dangerous upgrades by comparing to Leela's body modifications
jokes.punchline: Leela points out the safety difference between breasts and ship modifications
jokes.explanation: Absurd comparison that's both character-specific and genuinely funny - 'bazooms' is a great word choice

jokes.text: Done broke my spine. Nice rack, though. / Thank you, Scruffy.
jokes.setup: Scruffy is seriously injured from crash
jokes.punchline: Politely compliments Leela's breasts despite broken spine, she thanks him politely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment - his unflappable politeness in any situation is expertly deployed here

jokes.text: You wrecked my dear ship, Bessie?! I just finished naming her.
jokes.setup: Professor is distraught about his wrecked ship
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally just named it moments ago
jokes.explanation: Shows Professor's tendency to dramatic overreaction - his 'dear ship' attachment is literally brand new

jokes.text: Fry did it!
jokes.setup: Someone needs to be blamed for the crash
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately blames Fry as predicted earlier
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that pays off the earlier setup perfectly

jokes.text: Here I comes to save the days.
jokes.setup: Professor arrives to help with the situation
jokes.punchline: Mangles the heroic 'here I come to save the day' phrase
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate verbal fumble - Professor's age showing through grammar

jokes.text: Oh, what the heck-- a hot rod!
jokes.setup: Professor just said 'tepid rod'
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects himself to the proper phrase
jokes.explanation: Quick payoff to the previous malapropism, shows Professor catching his mistake

jokes.text: Ah... time for the mornings meltsdowns.
jokes.setup: Sal observing the junkyard routine
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of daily 'meltdowns' as normal routine
jokes.explanation: Good Sal characterization - his casual attitude toward workplace chaos

jokes.text: I'll pay you back, I swear. / Yeah, yeah, I heards its befores.
jokes.setup: Professor promises to pay for parts
jokes.punchline: Sal's weary acceptance shows this is a recurring issue
jokes.explanation: Shows history between characters - Sal knows Professor's payment promises are worthless

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Whats the...? / In your face, sucker! Also, I'll send you a check for the parts I stole!
jokes.setup: Sal is confused as Professor races past
jokes.punchline: Professor maintains politeness even while stealing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Professor's polite criminality is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Okays, ya honest young punk!
jokes.setup: Professor just stole parts but promised to pay
jokes.punchline: Sal calls him 'honest' despite the theft
jokes.explanation: Sal's twisted logic - theft with promise of payment equals honesty

jokes.text: Step off, you greasers! I was street-racing back when ships still ran on moonwater!
jokes.setup: Young racers call Professor slow
jokes.punchline: Professor claims ancient street racing experience with absurd fuel type
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of Professor's age and sci-fi setting - 'moonwater' is wonderfully absurd

jokes.text: Your mouth just wrote a PayPal transfer request that your butt has insufficient funds to honor!
jokes.setup: Classic 'your mouth wrote a check your ass can't cash' setup
jokes.punchline: Updates it with modern digital payment terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modernization of classic phrase - PayPal reference is perfectly anachronistic for future setting

jokes.text: Yo, it is on! / Before that, it was off.
jokes.setup: Racer declares 'it is on' (the challenge)
jokes.punchline: Professor takes it literally about the previous state
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor literal-mindedness - perfectly timed and economical

jokes.text: Twice around Central Park. Loser has to make the winner's Medicare copayments for a year!
jokes.setup: Setting stakes for the race
jokes.punchline: Stakes are elderly-specific Medicare payments instead of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific stakes - shows Professor's age and financial priorities

jokes.text: Aw, man, I can't afford no Medicare copayments!
jokes.setup: Stakes were set as Medicare copayments
jokes.punchline: Young racer realizes he can't afford old person medical costs
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the generational gap in healthcare costs

jokes.text: Yo, Wrinkles, you got big glasses. I'm gonna call you the Professor. / What?! No one calls me that!
jokes.setup: Racer gives Professor a nickname based on appearance
jokes.punchline: Professor objects to being called his actual title/name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - the street name happens to be his actual professional title

jokes.text: Girl, he don't own you! / Yeah, but he saved me from my abusive father. Not physically abusive; verbally. It's a long story I can't get into while we're spinning out.
jokes.setup: Typical racing movie romance dialogue
jokes.punchline: Overly specific backstory delivered during dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Parodies racing movie tropes with overly specific trauma details at inappropriate time

jokes.text: Ow! Paper cut!
jokes.setup: High-speed chase with crashes and danger
jokes.punchline: Character complains about minor paper cut amid major chaos
jokes.explanation: Classic priorities reversal - minor injury gets attention during major danger

jokes.text: Ironic!
jokes.setup: Police have been chasing these racers unsuccessfully for years
jokes.punchline: Officer notes the irony as they crash while being chased
jokes.explanation: Simple observation humor - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yo, Professor, if your ship's so dope, why are those cops on your tail like my dad's verbal abuse on my psyche.
jokes.setup: Questioning Professor's boasting about his ship
jokes.punchline: Compares police pursuit to persistent psychological trauma
jokes.explanation: Continues the verbal abuse running gag with creative metaphor usage

jokes.text: Sayonara, Tokyo driftwood!
jokes.setup: Professor preparing to escape police with dimensional drift
jokes.punchline: Mangles 'Tokyo Drift' reference into 'Tokyo driftwood'
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - attempts to be cool but mangles pop culture reference

jokes.text: I can't believe we lost 'em again. I need a drink. / Nah. We're on the job. Let's just go pepper-spray some homeless families.
jokes.setup: Police frustrated about losing the racers again
jokes.punchline: Suggests casual police brutality against vulnerable populations as alternative
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's more shock value than clever - relies on edginess over craft

jokes.text: In my case, it was verbal abuse. / That's in the rearview mirror, baby. It wasn't what he said that hurt, it was what was left unsaid.
jokes.setup: Continuing the verbal abuse backstory
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-profound analysis of psychological damage from unspoken words
jokes.explanation: Develops the running gag with mock-therapeutic insight - somewhat overwrought

jokes.text: Again, no idea what that means.
jokes.setup: Street racers ask Professor to join their crew
jokes.punchline: Professor admits he doesn't understand their slang
jokes.explanation: Consistent character beat about generational language gap

jokes.text: Our safe new ship has arrived, and it's gonna knock your socks on... where they belong.
jokes.setup: Hermes announcing the new safe ship
jokes.punchline: Starts to say 'knock your socks off' but corrects to safety-appropriate version
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing safety obsession overriding natural speech patterns

jokes.text: Hey, wait. If I'm backwards, I can't see out the windshield. / There is no windshield.
jokes.setup: Fry worried about sitting backwards
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'safe' ship has no windshield at all
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of safety absurdity - removes essential safety feature in name of safety

jokes.text: At least there's a cup holder. / That's an arm holder.
jokes.setup: Fry finds something positive about the ship
jokes.punchline: Even basic amenities are safety-focused rather than convenience
jokes.explanation: Continues safety obsession theme - economical and well-timed

jokes.text: ♪ Bender is bored, Bender is bored ♪ ♪ Bender, Bender, Bender... ♪
jokes.setup: Safe ship journey is boring
jokes.punchline: Bender creates annoying song about his boredom
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender attention-seeking behavior - perfectly in character

jokes.text: Like my aunt's neighbor is a hoarder. Stack of magazines fell on her cat. It's hard, you know?
jokes.setup: Street racers rap about their problems
jokes.punchline: Extremely mundane domestic problem treated as serious trauma
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor - treating trivial neighbor drama as deep personal struggle

jokes.text: Ain't no thing but a hyperchicken wing.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining dimensional technology
jokes.punchline: Attempts cool slang but adds sci-fi prefix to 'chicken wing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor moment - trying to be cool but making it unnecessarily scientific

jokes.text: But we didn't have an adventure. This is a delivery company. We need less adventures, not more adventures.
jokes.setup: Fry complains about boring, safe delivery
jokes.punchline: Professor points out adventures are actually bad for business
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the show's adventure formula. Professor's business logic vs entertainment needs.

jokes.text: Yo, soccer mom, speed it up! Move that lunch bucket!
jokes.setup: Street racer encounters slow vehicle
jokes.punchline: Calls the Planet Express ship a 'lunch bucket'
jokes.explanation: 'Lunch bucket' is a good insult for the boxy safe ship design

jokes.text: Because unlike you, I'm cool. I drive fast late at night when I should be sleeping.
jokes.setup: Professor defending his coolness
jokes.punchline: Describes reckless behavior in terms of disrupted bedtime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor cool-attempt - framing rebellion in elderly concerns about sleep schedule

jokes.text: That one-eyed housewife called you out! / When and where, PTA?
jokes.setup: Racers encouraging fight between Professor and Leela
jokes.punchline: Professor calls Leela 'PTA' assuming she's a suburban mom
jokes.explanation: Good character assumption - Professor sees safe ship and assumes suburban mom stereotype

jokes.text: Möbius drag strip, man.
jokes.setup: Question about the strange racing venue
jokes.punchline: Reveals mathematical topology used as race track
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi concept - using mathematical topology for racing is genuinely creative

jokes.text: One more lap! / Nah, half a lap. You forgot, on a Möbius strip, two laps is one lap.
jokes.setup: Confusion about race progress
jokes.punchline: Professor explains Möbius strip mathematical properties mid-race
jokes.explanation: Pays off the mathematical setup with actual topology education during action sequence

jokes.text: You kids and your topology.
jokes.setup: Professor just explained the Möbius strip
jokes.punchline: Dismisses advanced mathematics as youth trend despite being a scientist
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Professor dismisses his own field of expertise as generational fad

jokes.text: Now who's in the lead?! / Seriously, I have no idea.
jokes.setup: Professor boasts after dimensional drift move
jokes.punchline: Admits he genuinely doesn't know due to dimensional confusion
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of heroic moment - honest confusion undercuts dramatic gesture

jokes.text: No, no. I choose to believe they're alive in some other dimension. Screaming in agony. / I hope so.
jokes.setup: Concern about whether Professor and Leela died in crash
jokes.punchline: Optimistic belief they're suffering horribly elsewhere instead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that subverts normal concern - preferring suffering to death

jokes.text: Why do I look like a stupid cartoon character? / I feel like I lost weight. All of it.
jokes.setup: Characters notice their 2D appearance
jokes.punchline: Professor realizes he lost all his weight (third dimension)
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about animation style plus literal interpretation of dimensional physics

jokes.text: Oh, no! Like Flatland? / Call it what you will. The point is, the new Shrek movie won't look nearly as good.
jokes.setup: Leela recognizes classic sci-fi reference to dimensional story
jokes.punchline: Professor focuses on pop culture movie experience implications
jokes.explanation: Good priorities joke - focuses on entertainment concerns over existential crisis

jokes.text: I've been... emotionally scarred by years of unspoken verbal abuse.
jokes.setup: Character trying to be honest with new co-workers
jokes.punchline: Continues the verbose description of family trauma theme
jokes.explanation: Continues established character trait but adding less each time

jokes.text: Well, that's the same, but other things are different.
jokes.setup: Professor demonstrates 2D physics by dropping marble
jokes.punchline: Admits the demonstration showed nothing different while claiming other things are
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - failed scientific demonstration with weak rationalization.

jokes.text: Ow! / Ow! / You can't step past in this dimension. You have to step around.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to step past Leela in 2D world
jokes.punchline: Physical impossibility of passing in two dimensions causes collision
jokes.explanation: Clever physical comedy based on actual mathematical limitations of 2D space

jokes.text: Fourth dimension?! I can't picture that! You're dumb.
jokes.setup: Professor explains fourth dimensional observation
jokes.punchline: 2D being responds exactly like people do to higher dimensional concepts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel - 2D being reacts to 4D like humans react to higher dimensions

jokes.text: Hey, how come I can't swallow?
jokes.setup: Fry tries to eat 2D food
jokes.punchline: Discovers digestive systems don't work in 2D
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the scientific explanation - practical discovery of 2D limitations

jokes.text: Third dimension? I can't picture that! You're dumb.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions returning to third dimension
jokes.punchline: King repeats exact same response about dimensional confusion
jokes.explanation: Consistent character response but less impact on repeat

jokes.text: There's nothing funny about science! If there can be an X-axis and a Y-axis, why not a Z-axis? He's opening our minds to new ideas! Kill him!
jokes.setup: Professor explains three-dimensional concept
jokes.punchline: 2D beings go from intellectual appreciation to violent rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - intellectual curiosity immediately turns to fear-based violence

jokes.text: Oh, if only Minx could see that. Then she'd see me as more than just a father figure.
jokes.setup: Professor planning heroic dimensional escape
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate romantic interest in much younger woman
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate creepiness - Professor's inappropriate romantic delusions

jokes.text: Two point pi!
jokes.setup: Counting dimensions as they escape
jokes.punchline: Uses mathematical constant pi as dimension measurement
jokes.explanation: Clever math humor - using pi as dimension measurement fits Professor's character and scientific background.

jokes.text: My bowels returned at a bad time!
jokes.setup: Crew escaping 2D dimension in dramatic chase
jokes.punchline: Fry's digestive system returns during inconvenient moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with terrible timing - body functions return at worst possible moment

jokes.text: I'm gonna burns them ups.
jokes.setup: Bender seeing the crashed characters
jokes.punchline: Casual threat to burn them, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender casual violence - perfectly in character response to any situation

jokes.text: Hello? Dad?!
jokes.setup: Phone rings after apparent resolution
jokes.punchline: It's the abusive father she's been talking about all episode
jokes.explanation: Great callback that pays off the running verbal abuse subplot. The timing suggests ongoing trauma despite apparent resolution.
